preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde's chances stand at 29%. The gap between the two sides is modest—a 12-percentage-point margin—suggesting the teams occupy similar competitive territory despite Saudi Arabia's edge.

A draw carries meaningful weight in the model's forecast at 31%, indicating genuine uncertainty about which team will prevail. The outcome space is relatively balanced across all three results: no single scenario dominates decisively. This distribution points to a competitive match where the outcome could plausibly fall any direction.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably on Cape Verde's chances. Books price them at 36%, a 7-percentage-point premium over the model's 29%. Markets and Polymarket show closer alignment to each other on the draw (both near 26%) and Saudi Arabia's win odds (both around 38–39%). The dashboard does not determine which assessment is correct; the discrepancy warrants monitoring as match day approaches.
